content.text:
Summary / current status:

- I searched the mesh for an existing pricing container (queries: @pricing, @Pricing, @graph/Pricing, nodeType:Pricing) and did not find any.
- I attempted to create the Pricing container and two pricing nodes (Tesla, Nestle) and lightweight company reference nodes using the mesh UpdateContent API, but those UpdateContent calls returned platform errors: "Types JsonElement could not be mapped to data source". Because of these API mapping errors the nodes were not created.

Next action taken:
- I have delegated the full, exact creation task and instructions to the Navigator agent (including the fields, audit metadata, linking to companies, and the requirement to NavigateTo/display the created pricing nodes). Navigator now has the handoff and will perform the changes in the mesh and return the results (paths/IDs and visual displays).

Details I used:
- effectiveDate set to today: 2026-02-19
- audit.createdBy: Navigator
- audit.createdAt: 2026-02-19T19:55:00Z (UTC)
- price left null and status set to "Draft"
- currency default "USD"
- product default "General"

What I need from you (if you want to expedite or change defaults):
- Confirm you want USD and product "General", or provide product/currency/price values now.
- If you prefer, I can retry creating the nodes directly (but the API mapping error may persist). Delegating to Navigator is the recommended escalation to handle mesh API specifics.

I’ll report back with the full created node paths/IDs and display each pricing node as soon as Navigator completes the changes.
